original_query: SELECT COALESCE(SUM("EXPR0"), 0), "T2" FROM (SELECT "t5"."T2", COUNT(CASE WHEN "EMP1"."DEPTNO" = 0 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS "EXPR0" FROM "EMP" AS "EMP1", (VALUES (1)) AS "t5" ("T2") GROUP BY "t5"."T2" UNION ALL SELECT "t8"."T2", COUNT(CASE WHEN "EMP2"."DEPTNO" = 0 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS "EXPR0" FROM "EMP" AS "EMP2", (VALUES (2)) AS "t8" ("T2") GROUP BY "t8"."T2") AS "t11" GROUP BY "T2"
rewritten_query:
SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN "EMP"."DEPTNO" = 0 THEN 1 END) AS "EXPR0", t."T2"
FROM "EMP"
CROSS JOIN (VALUES (1), (2)) AS t("T2")
GROUP BY t."T2"

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Eliminated UNION ALL by using a single CROSS JOIN with VALUES to generate T2 values, reducing the number of table scans from two to one
Simplified the CASE expression by removing the redundant ELSE NULL clause since COUNT already ignores NULL values
Removed unnecessary COALESCE function since COUNT never returns NULL
Eliminated redundant outer GROUP BY and SUM operations since each T2 value already has exactly one row in the result set
